concept.title: Checking the Nozzles Using the Product Control Panel
concept.shortdesc: You can check the print head nozzles using the control panel on your product.

conbody.p: You can check the print head nozzles using the control panel on your product.

ol.li: Make sure there are no errors on the LCD screen and the output tray is extended.
Load a few sheets of plain paper in the product.

li.p: Press the home button, if necessary.
Press the start button to print. The nozzle check pattern is printed.
Check the printed pattern to see if there are gaps in the lines.

section.title: Result

ul.li: If there are no gaps, the print head is clean. Select Noand press the OKbutton.
If there are gaps or the pattern is faint, select Yesand press the OKbutton. Follow the instructions on the screen to clean the print head.
If most lines are missing or not printed, most of the nozzles are clogged. Select Noand press the

section.p: OKbutton, then run a Power Cleaning. See the link below.

If you don’t see any improvement after cleaning the print head up to 3 times, turn the product off and wait at least 12 hours. Then run a nozzle check and try cleaning the print head again. If quality still does not improve, run a Power Cleaning.
